name: Balangan (Chinese: 巴兰干; Indonesian: Pantai Balangan)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: Approximately 14-16 km from Ngurah Rai (DPS) Airport (around 30-40 min by car)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~65-80 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: ~15-20 min by scooter
wave_type: Left-hand reef break / point break with multiple sections; long fast walls and occasional barrels on bigger days
skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced primary; Beginners only on very small high-tide days; Pro on overhead-double-overhead
wave_height_range: 1-10 ft (works from 1 ft up to double overhead); sweet spot 3-6 ft for intermediates
bottom_type: Reef (flat coral shelf); shallow inside section that can be exposed at low tide
best_season: Dry season, April/May to October (peak: June-September); most consistent in July
best_tide: Mid to high tide (above ~1.4 m) for safest surf; works all tides but low exposes shallow reef and walking access becomes tricky
best_wind: Offshore from East to East-Southeast (E-SE trade winds)
best_swell_direction: Southwest (SW) primary; South (S) and SSW also produce clean lines
swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 during dry season - one of the most consistent Bukit breaks; ~99% rideable in July [uncertain]
wave_length_meters: Typically 100-200 m rides; on rare large clean days, multiple sections can link for 300+ m
wave_speed_rating: Fast - flat reef means most waves run fast and don't barrel often except on bigger powerful swells
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high - shifting peaks require positioning; drop is manageable but section ahead breaks quickly demanding immediate trim line
secondary_peaks: Yes - main outside peak plus shifting inside sections; on bigger days outside lineup pushes further out, more peaks open along the reef
peak_crowd_level: 4/5 - popular and busy in July-August peak season; spreads across long reef so early sessions or paddling wide can find space
access: Drive to Balangan Beach via Jalan Pantai Balangan in Jimbaran/Pecatu; descend cliff stairs (~150 steps) to the beach; walk along the sand to the lineup; at low tide walk across the reef carefully near the headland
paddle_out_method: Paddle out from the sand near the headland to use the keyhole/channel; at lower tides walk along the reef edge to a deeper jump-off point; booties recommended at low tide
entry_fee: Entrance fee ~IDR 5,000 per person (sometimes waived); parking IDR 3,000 motorbike / IDR 5,000 car
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 am) when winds are lightest and crowds thinnest; sunset session 4-6pm
best_time_of_day: Before 8am to avoid surf-school groups and tourist crowds; after 4pm when day-trippers leave
nearby_warung: Multiple beachfront warungs along the sand: Froggy Warung, La Joya warung, La Brisa, Flapjacks, fresh coconut and grilled fish stalls; cliff-top cafes like Sunday Beach Club and La Joya Biu Biu
alternative_spot_within_10min: Dreamland (easier beach break), Bingin (mid-tide left reef), Padang Padang (high tide), Impossibles (longer paddle), Nyang Nyang on bigger swells
hazards: Sharp shallow coral reef especially at low tide, strong currents on big days, shallow middle section with hidden boils, occasional sea urchins, cliff staircase slippery when wet
marine_hazards: Sea urchins (bulu babi) on inside reef, occasional jellyfish in shoulder seasons, no significant stonefish reports; treat reef cuts with antiseptic and oral antibiotics if deep
reef_cut_severity_index: Medium-high - flat coral reef with sharp ridges; mid-section can be deceptively shallow at low tide and has cut many surfers and dinged many boards
rip_current_pattern: Channel current runs along the reef edge toward the outside; sweeping rip near the headland can pull surfers north; identifiable by foam lines moving offshore
lineup_etiquette: Respect priority and rotation; locals expect courteous behaviour; don't paddle around to snake the peak; greet other surfers; share waves on crowded days
localism_intensity: Low to medium - generally welcoming due to tourist popularity but can tense up on perfect crowded days with disrespectful visitors
respect_protocol: Wait your turn, no dropping in, acknowledge local surfers, tip warungs/parking attendants, do not bring glass or leave trash on the sand/reef
resident_local_legends: Many Bukit/Jimbaran-based locals surf here including up-and-coming groms; not specifically associated with one iconic figure [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Respect daily Balinese offerings (canang sari) on the sand and at cliff shrines; Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - beach closed and no surfing; cover up when leaving the beach via villages
medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (closest 24/7 with surf injury experience), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua, Siloam Hospital Denpasar
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu ~4-5 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~10 km; Siloam Denpasar ~17 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; informal help between surfers; lifeguard presence inconsistent [uncertain]
emergency_contact: Indonesia emergency: 112; Bali Search and Rescue (Basarnas): +62 361 9351111; Unicare Pecatu: +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: Balangan Surf School (on the beach), local instructors with softboards on the sand, Rip Curl School of Surf (Dreamland branch nearby), Odyssey Surf School
board_rental_availability: Available on the beach from local stalls; softboards, longboards and shortboards; from ~IDR 50,000-150,000/day depending on board
surf_photographer_available: Yes - cliff-top land photographers regularly shoot Balangan; some water photographers during peak season
photographer_recommendation: Local freelancers offer photo sets ~IDR 200,000-400,000; advance booking via Instagram common [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Balangan Surf Resort, La Joya Balangan, Balangan Paradise Hostel, The Palm Grove, Mu Bali Bingin, numerous homestays and surf camps in Pecatu/Jimbaran
webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/balangan/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d3a (Surfline cam, may require subscription); https://balicams.com cam for Balangan [uncertain - availability]
drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for personal recreational use; avoid flying over crowds and resort properties; commercial filming requires permits
coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu, Drifter Surf Cafe, Suka Espresso (Uluwatu area) all ~15-20 min by scooter
avg_lesson_price: ~IDR 350,000 for 1.5-hour beginner lesson on small days; private/intermediate lessons IDR 600,000-1,000,000
board_rental_daily: IDR 50,000-150,000 per day (softboard cheapest); shortboards IDR 100,000-200,000
water_quality_rating: Good in dry season - clear blue water; can degrade slightly in wet season due to runoff and floating debris
annual_competition_window: Hosts regional and grom contests occasionally; not a primary WSL CT venue; surrounding Bukit competitions June-September [uncertain]
community_events: Sunset gatherings at beachfront warungs; nearby Single Fin Sunday (Uluwatu) is the regional weekly gathering; informal sunset surfs and beach barbecues
